mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months signals rising inflation expectations, leading to a decrease in the purchasing power of consumers and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or CPI growth does not lead to a decrease in consumer spending.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to increased wage pressure and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor markets are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the past 6 months signals an increase in supply chain activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or INDPRO growth does not lead to an increase in supply chain activity.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) over the past quarter signals an increase in aggregate demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in increasing demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the past 3 months signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or FEDFUNDS growth does not lead to a decrease in borrowing costs.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the number of payroll employees (PAYEMS) over the past 3 months signals an increase in labor market activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in increasing labor market activity periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the number of housing starts (HOUST) over the past 3 months signals an increase in housing market activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in increasing housing market activity periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
